rows.label: tev079201_JNK0010
rows.name: Inquire

rows.label: tev079201_SEL0011
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran about humanity.

rows.label: tev079201_JNK0020
rows.name: Discuss

rows.label: tev079201_SEL0021
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran about recent developments.

rows.label: tev079201_SEL0031
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran about collectibles.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_SEL1021
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran about recent developments.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_SEL1031
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran what he intends to do next.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_SEL2021
rows.name: Ask Hoskeran about recent developments.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0001
rows.name:
So you humans moved your souls into
mechanical bodies call Blue Bloods
and traveled to Mira? *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0002
rows.name:
That was an bold move
for certain! *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0003
rows.name:
But why not go all the way? *fsssh* Why maintain
purely emotional functions that can only interfere
with your ability to explore a new world?[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0004
rows.name:
Plenty of Growth members have mechanized their own
bodies, but I've never seen any of them add the
functionality to cry as you humans have. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0005
rows.name:
Was it perhaps some sort of...joke?
*fsssh* If so, I'm surprised at how
far you would go for amusement.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0010
rows.name:
*fsssh* When I'm steering my trailer through
Primordial Wilderness in search of voltant,
I occasionally encounter BLADEs.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0011
rows.name:
Sometimes they're battling indigens... Sometimes
they're desperately pounding strange poles into
the dirt... *fsssh* Yes, I've seen all kinds.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0012
rows.name:
But whenever they see an indigen
attacking my vehicle, they almost
always come to help. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0013
rows.name:
The other day, they even escorted me to a
pleasant place called a "base camp" so that
I could rest and recuperate. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0014
rows.name:
I am most grateful. *fsssh* The next time I feel
an urge to skip work, I will allow myself to
indulge in a nap at one of your "base camps."[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0019
rows.name:
My explorations have confirmed
that there is voltant to be found
throughout this planet. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0020
rows.name:
In White Woods, to the north, there blooms
a flower known as the antorus rose that
contains a potent voltant within. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0021
rows.name:
Should you continue north to more distant lands,
you'll find belzadozebu scallions, which contain a
voltant strong enough to bring down giant indigens.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0022
rows.name:
*fsssh* Perhaps your journeys will take
you to such distant lands someday.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG0023
rows.name:
If so, I would be honored to
receive a large quantity of toxic
items as a souvenir! *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1001
rows.name:
I never imagined that the masked
man who stole the voltant would
turn out to be Homer. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1002
rows.name:
Although now that I think back upon it, I do
vaguely recall him asking where our voltant
was stored during one of his lessons. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1003
rows.name:
At the time, I figured he wanted that information
in order to craft some sort of new metaphor
or something. *fsssh* What a shame.[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1004
rows.name:
Well, I suppose it wouldn't hurt to pay
a visit to this "prison" place where he
is being held and hear him out. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1005
rows.name:
And if he were to grace me with
another of his thrilling metaphors,
all the better! *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1010
rows.name:
I tried using this FrontierNet that
your people have installed. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1011
rows.name:
I was told to develop a route that
would traverse all of Mira so that we
might recover its voltant. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1012
rows.name:
It is an incredible hassle. Metaphorically speaking,
it is a hassle on the magnitude of returning to
here from the lowest strata of NLA. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG1013
rows.name:
If I were to truly pursue such a plan, I would need to
devote every ounce of strength I possess to finding good
napping points for each step of the route. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG2001
rows.name:
The more I think about it, the more
I wonder what Homer was doing there
in the first place. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG2002
rows.name:
Perhaps he was on a training journey
to heighten his talent for crafting
brilliantly clear metaphors? *fsssh*[ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG2003
rows.name:
Hmm. Risking one's life in pursuit of
training... *fsssh* It's just like the
baibai beetle said to be found in White Woods![ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG2004
rows.name:
I should have expected no less of the
professor. *fsssh* I must work harder on
my own training so as not to fall behind![ST:wait ]

rows.label: tev079201_MSG2005
rows.name:
While the professor is still pursuing his training
in that "prison," I must ask him to teach me the
deepest secrets of crafting metaphors. *fsssh*[ST:wait ]
